description: A hawker stall serving laksa, offering the best and most delicious experience.
The Aerotel Transit Hotel is located on the airside of Terminal 1. Solo or double rooms are available, and they also have an outdoor swimming pool, fitness center, and more.
The Ambassador Transit Hotel is located on the airside of Terminals 2 and 3. Clean and comfortable rooms, but slightly overpriced for what you get.

Arab Street is a popular tourist spot, offering a chance to explore the local culture and try a variety of food options.

A neighborhood with a rich history and culture, offering a great experience and a must-visit destination.
A restaurant serving prawn mee, offering a great experience and a must-try dish.
The Blossom Lounge is located in Terminal 4 of the airport.
The Butterfly Garden is a nice little tropical habitat with a grotto waterfall and more than 1,000 tropical butterflies from about 40 species.
The Canopy Bridge is a transparent glass bridge suspended 23 meters above ground. If you’ve never walked on a glass bottom bridge before, this is a great place to try it!
The top floor of Changi Jewel (at Level 5) is home to a 14,000 square meter recreational area called the Canopy Park, which has a bunch of fun family-friendly activities.

The Singapore airport is a vacation in itself, and many of the best things to do in the airport are free! Also known as the Changi airport, it’s often ranked as the best in the world, with a giant indoor waterfall, butterfly garden, rooftop pool, the Jewel mall, and other fun sights and activities.
The Changi Point Coastal Walk is a scenic walk along the coast, offering beautiful views and a chance to explore the area.
A restaurant serving Singaporean food, offering a great experience and a convenient location.
A neighborhood with a rich history and culture, offering a great experience and a must-visit destination.

The Chinatown Food Center is a local icon, offering a variety of Singaporean, Malaysian, and Chinese-fusion meals at affordable prices, with the option to fill up on tasty eats for about $5 SGD ($3.75 USD).
The Cloud Forest is located in Gardens by the Bay and can be easily accessed from the Marina Bay Sands hotel.
I indulged in the biggest buffet I have ever seen in my life
A restaurant serving Asian cuisines, offering the greatest buffet and a great value for money.
The Crowne Plaza Airport Hotel is located by Terminal 3. It’s a luxurious hotel with runway views and a swimming pool, fitness center, and more. Best option if you can afford it.
The DC Comics Super Heroes Café is one of the many restaurants located in the Marina Bay Sands hotel.
The Digital Light Canvas is a unique artistic experience located near the Marina Bay Sands hotel.
Dream Lodge is a hostel located on the hip Lavender Street, with easy access to three MRT stations and a variety of amenities.
The East Coast Park is a must-see attraction, with a beautiful coastal walk and a variety of activities to enjoy.

The Gardens by the Bay were the highlight of my Singapore trip, with the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest costing money to get into, but the Gardens and Supertree Grove are free to enjoy all day, opening at 5 AM.

Walking through the futuristic forests felt like walking into another planet. This popular attraction is always busy so I recommend getting there about 15 minutes early so you’re among the first ones to purchase tickets for the Cloud Forest, which run at $28 SGD (about $21 USD) per person.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest open up at 9 a.m., so I recommend getting there about 15 minutes early so you’re among the first ones to purchase tickets, which run at $28 SGD (about $21 USD) per person.
The HSBC Rain Vortex is the world’s tallest indoor waterfall, cascading down 40 meters and through 7 stories. The waterfall is free to visit, and highly photogenic at all times of day.
Haji Lane is a popular tourist spot, known for its vibrant atmosphere and a variety of shopping and dining options.
Haw Par Villa is a temple and a popular tourist destination, offering a chance to learn about Chinese culture and history.
I slurped on my favorite Prawn Mee noodles and chili crab
The Hedge Maze at Canopy Park is Singapore’s biggest hedge maze.

Hotel Kai is a hotel with a dream location, offering single rooms starting at $80 USD and a personal Wi-Fi device for 24/7 connection all over the country.

A budget hotel located in the center of the historic Civic District, offering a great value for money and a 4G personal Wi-Fi device.
Hotel Mono is a boutique hotel located in the heart of the city, with ongoing deals and a variety of rooms, including single, double, and family rooms.
Hotel Yan is a hotel with an industrial-chic aesthetic, located next to Little India and offering double rooms for around $120 USD.
The infinity pool is really the crown jewel of the Marina Bay Sands in Singapore. Located 57 levels above the city, the Sands SkyPark offers breathtaking views of Singapore in every direction.

The Jewel mall, which has almost 300 food and shopping outlets of its own. You can find everything here, including luxury goods, jewelry, watches, clothing, shoes, electronics, toys, gifts, souvenirs, chocolate, wine, and more!

Joo Chiat is a neighborhood in Singapore, known for its Peranakan culture and a variety of food options, including the famous Joo Chiat Road.

A historic neighborhood with a rich culture, offering a great experience and a must-visit destination.

Little India is a neighborhood in Singapore, known for its vibrant atmosphere and a variety of Indian food options.

A neighborhood with a rich history and culture, offering a great experience and a must-visit destination.
A beautiful bay with a blend of Malaysian, Indian, Chinese, Arab and English cultures.

Marina Bay Sands is a luxury hotel in Singapore, known for its stunning architecture and a variety of amenities, including a rooftop pool and a casino.

A stunning architectural icon. If you have ever seen pictures of Singapore, you have likely seen shots of the Marina Bay Sands hotel in the city’s scape. This place is easily one of the most famous hotels in Asia, if not the world. An architectural wonder in the heart of Singapore, this is one place that will definitely captivate you as you begin exploring the city.

The city’s skyline was absolutely gorgeous from up there. To experience the famous pool, you have to be a hotel guest.
An observation deck with a great view of the cityscape, offering a unique experience and a must-visit destination.
The Mirror Maze can be fun for kids too.
A restaurant serving Japanese food tapas style, located in the hipster hood of Tanjong Pagar.
It’ll set you back $23 SGD ($17 USD), but seeing Singapore from the deck is so worth it!
The Orchid Garden has more than 700 orchids from 30 varieties, arranged according to their colors and shapes.
The Plaza Premium Lounge is located in Terminal 1 of the airport, while the Blossom Lounge is in Terminal 4. These are both located in transit (airside).
A restaurant serving Singaporean staples, offering a fresh take and a great experience.

Sentosa Island is a popular tourist destination with beautiful beaches and a variety of attractions, including the Gardens by the Bay and the Supertree Grove.

This little island off the southern coast of Singapore, has theme parks, golf courses, spas and a gorgeous, sandy coast!

An island with a variety of theme parks, golf courses, and beaches, offering a great experience and a must-visit destination.
The Shiseido Forest Valley, which is covered with green foliage, trees, and walking paths.
A 158-year-old tropical oasis, offering a great experience and a must-visit destination.
Located 57 levels above the city, the Sands SkyPark offers breathtaking views of Singapore in every direction. From the top of the world, you can swim to the edge of the pool or lounge in a jacuzzi.
The Spectra Light & Water Show is a nightly show that can be viewed from the Marina Bay Sands hotel.
A mosque with a rich history and culture, offering a great experience and a must-visit destination.
The Sunflower Garden is a small garden scattered throughout the transit areas of the Singapore airport.

The Supertree Grove is a free attraction in the Gardens by the Bay, with the option to explore the upper walkway between the trees for $8 SGD ($6 USD) per person.

The Supertree Grove is free to walk around in at anytime!

The Supertree Groves are located in Gardens by the Bay and can be easily accessed from the Marina Bay Sands hotel.
Terminal 1 is home to the Water Lily Garden and the Aerotel Transit Hotel.
Terminal 2 is home to the Sunflower Garden and the Orchid Garden.
Terminal 3 is home to the Butterfly Garden and the Slide.
Terminal 4 is home to the Blossom Lounge.
The Bohemian Chic is a hostel offering stylish social accommodations in the bustling Chinatown area of Singapore.

The Colony serves every meal of the day, including afternoon tea from Monday to Saturday and a Champagne brunch on Sundays. It is a luxury buffet at a reasonable price, with a smart-casual dress code.
A restaurant serving Indian food, offering the best flavors and a great experience.
A hotel with a perfect location on the waterfront, offering a swanky rooftop bar and first-class service.
The Pod is a hostel offering capsule hotels, with female, male, and mixed dormitories starting at $25 USD, and a note that the capsules feel more like bunk beds than other capsules.
The Quarters Hostel is a chic and comfortable hostel, popular among budget travelers and offering a variety of amenities.
A waterfront area with a great view and a must-visit destination, offering a variety of restaurants and bars.
A hotel located in the center of the trendy Orchard District, offering a great experience and a 4-star rating.

The Ritz-Carlton, Millenia Singapore is a luxury hotel in Singapore, known for its stunning views of the Marina Bay and a variety of amenities, including a rooftop pool and a spa.

The Ritz-Carlton Millenia Singapore is the best hotel in Singapore, according to Travel & Leisure. The hotel has a jaw-dropping view of the famous Singapore skyline over Marina Bay. The rooms are among the biggest in Singapore, with options ranging from 776 to 2,358 square feet. The Club Premier rooms offer separate bedroom and living room spaces, marvelous marble bathrooms, and striking views of the bay and the city.

The Ritz-Carlton, Millenia Singapore is another hotel in Singapore, mentioned in the related posts section.
A 4-star hotel with an art-deco style, offering a captivating old-world charm and a convenient location.

The Slide is Singapore’s tallest slide, and it’s also the tallest slide in an airport anywhere in the world! It’s a curvy slide that is 12 meters tall (four stories high), and should be lots of fun for kids and kids at heart.
The Southern Ridges offer a breathtaking view and a chance to explore the natural beauty of Singapore.
A hawker stall serving chicken rice, offering a great experience and a must-try dish.
The Water Lily Garden is a small garden scattered throughout the transit areas of the Singapore airport.
Wink Capsule is a hostel offering comfy capsule options, located in the buzzing Chinatown area of Singapore.
YOTELAIR Singapore Changi is located in the public area of the Changi Jewel (not airside), but within walking distance of Terminals 1, 2, and 3. Small but comfy double rooms.
